items.title: k-NN classification and regression
items.description: Labels or target values are aggregated from the k smallest-distance training points; Euclidean, Manhattan, Minkowski (tune p) and cosine (on embeddings) are common `metric` choices in scikit-learn and similar libraries.

items.title: Vector search and RAG
items.description: Embedding indexes rank documents by cosine similarity or L2 distance after normalization; metric choice determines which passages ground an LLM response.

items.title: Customer and image segmentation
items.description: k-means and hierarchical methods group records or pixel features by proximity; Euclidean distance on scaled feature vectors is the default geometry for k-means centroids.

items.title: Anomaly and outlier detection
items.description: Distance to k-th nearest neighbor or to a centroid flags atypical points; Manhattan can be robust to sparse outliers, while Mahalanobis distance (correlation-aware) appears in specialized detectors.
